sections.paragraph:
to spread out. (Arab. وضع to place, to spread out. Cognate roots are יָצַב, יָצַג, יָצַק.) Part. pass. יָצוּעַ subst. [simply taken as such in Thes.]

(1) a bed, a couch, Psa 63:7 132:3 Job 17:13 used of a marriage bed, Gen 49:4.

(2) a story, floor. Vulg. tabulatum. 1Ki 6:5, 1Ki 6:6, 10 1Ki 6:10(יָצִיעַ קרי). Const. fem. verse 1Ki 6:6(ter) and masc. verse 10 1Ki 6:10. In the temple of Solomon, loc. cit., this name was given to the three stories of side chambers (צְלָעֹות) on three of the sides of the temple, five cubits in height one above another; יָצוּעַ fem. Verse 1Ki 6:6is used of the single stories; in verses 1Ki 6:5, 10 1Ki 6:10(where it is masculine) it is used collectively of the whole of this part of the temple. Aug. Hirt (der Tempel Salomo’s, p. 24, 25), makes these three stories to have risen to the full height of the temple; in this he follows Josephus, but it is in contradiction to the express words of the Hebrew text, verse 18, וַיִּבֶן אֶת־הַיָּצוּעַ עַל־כָּל־הַבַּיִת חָמֵשׁ אַמֹּות קֹומָתֹו.

Hiphil הִצִּיעַ to spread out, to spread under. Psa 139:8, וְאַצִּיעָה שְׁאֹול “and (if) I spread out Hades beneath me,” i.e. make (it) my bed, Isa 58:5.

Hophal, pass. Isa 14:11, תַּחְתֶּיךָ יֻצַּע רִמָּה “the worms are spread under thee,” are for thy bed, Est 4:3 compare Isa 14:11.

Derivative מַצָּע.
